incident: Same address bridge: Bridge deposit on L1 and withdrawal on L2 use the identical address (canonical bridge default behavior)
label: H1_positive

incident: Same address bridge: L1 and L2 addresses both resolve to the same ENS name or are in the same ENS ownership graph
label: H1_positive

incident: Same address bridge: Smart contract wallet deployed via CREATE2 at the same address on both chains, linking via deterministic deployment
label: H1_positive

incident: Same address bridge: User followed recommendation -- Generate a fresh address on the destination L2 and specify it as the bridge recipient instead of using the default same-address behavior
label: H1_clean

incident: Amount correlation: L2 withdrawal amount equals L1 deposit minus the bridge fee, within a time window matching the bridge finality period
label: H2_positive

incident: Amount correlation: The bridged amount has fewer than 3 matches in the bridge deposit pool within 24 hours
label: H2_positive

incident: Amount correlation: Amount has more than 4 significant decimal places (not a round number), making it nearly unique
label: H2_positive

incident: Amount correlation: User followed recommendation -- Split a single bridge transfer into multiple round-number transfers across different time windows
label: H2_clean

incident: Bridge sequence fingerprint: L1 address bridges to the same ordered set of L2 chains as another known address cluster
label: H3_positive

incident: Bridge sequence fingerprint: Bridge events follow a periodic cadence (e.g., weekly, monthly) that matches a known user's pattern
label: H3_positive

incident: Bridge sequence fingerprint: Same address or cluster appears on 3+ L2s within a short time window, suggesting systematic bridging
label: H3_positive

incident: Bridge sequence fingerprint: User followed recommendation -- Vary the order and timing of bridges to different L2s to break recognizable patterns
label: H3_clean

incident: Gas funding post-bridge: L2 address's first ETH transfer comes from an address that received bridge withdrawals from a known L1 address
label: H4_positive

incident: Gas funding post-bridge: L2 address funded through exactly one intermediary that itself was funded by a bridge withdrawal
label: H4_positive

incident: Gas funding post-bridge: L2 address funded within minutes of a bridge withdrawal completing, suggesting automated pipeline
label: H4_positive

incident: Gas funding post-bridge: User followed recommendation -- Fund gas on L2 via a faucet, paymaster, or gasless transaction mechanism to avoid direct funding from bridged assets
label: H4_clean

incident: NFT/token bridge linkage: An ERC-721 or ERC-1155 token with a unique ID was bridged, creating a deterministic cross-chain link
label: H5_positive

incident: NFT/token bridge linkage: An ERC-20 token with fewer than 100 holders was bridged, making the transfer highly identifying
label: H5_positive

incident: NFT/token bridge linkage: A token amount so specific (e.g., 1,337.42069 USDC) that it effectively acts as an identifier across chains
label: H5_positive

incident: NFT/token bridge linkage: User followed recommendation -- Do not bridge NFTs directly if cross-chain privacy is needed. Sell on L1 and rebuy on L2 through a marketplace.
label: H5_clean
